assertions.description: INCORRECT: The analyzer emits a pwr_flag_warnings entry stating 'Power rail GND has power_in pins but no power_out or PWR_FLAG'. However, the schematic contains a power:PWR_FLAG symbol (#FLG01) placed at (97.79, 107.95) that is connected to the GND net via a GND power symbol at the same location. The PWR_FLAG is present and the warning should not fire.

INCORRECT: All three MMBT3904 transistor circuits have their collector connected directly to a relay coil pin (K1 pin 2, K2 pin 2, K3 pin 2 respectively). The analyzer correctly identifies the base resistors but reports load_type as 'other'. The load should be classified as 'relay' or 'inductive' since the collector load is a relay coil, which is the defining characteristic of this circuit.

D2, D4, D6 (US1A ultra-fast rectifiers) are placed as freewheeling/flyback protection diodes across each relay coil (K1, K2, K3 respectively). Their cathode (K pin) connects to VBUS and their anode connects to the transistor collector/relay coil pin, which is the standard anti-kickback topology. The signal_analysis.protection_devices list is empty — these should be detected as flyback or inductive-load protection diodes.
